title: Quantitative Reasoning, Estimation, and Problem Framing · Model Repair Bay
summary: Estimate attendance, supplies, time, and uncertainty for a neighborhood event before exact values are available. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a transparent event-planning estimate notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.
Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.
Translate a practical question into known quantities, unknown quantities, constraints, and operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Assumption Record for a transparent event-planning estimate notebook.
steps.why: The Assumption Record result for a transparent event-planning estimate notebook is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Assumption Record, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a transparent event-planning estimate notebook. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
